title: While Loops
description: Loop infinitely until a condition breaks.

slides.title: The Uncertain Loop 🌪️
slides.content:
A `for` loop is great when you know *exactly* how many times you want to repeat something (e.g., 5 times, or the length of an array).

But what if you want to loop until a specific event happens, and you don't know how long that will take? That's when you use a `while` loop.

slides.title: While Syntax
slides.content: A while loop takes a single condition. It runs the code block over and over as long as the condition remains `true`.

quizData.question: What happens if the condition in a `while` loop is `false` from the very beginning?

options.text: The loop runs exactly once and then stops.
The code inside the loop will never execute at all.

quizData.explanation: The condition is checked *before* every iteration. If it's initially false, the loop body is entirely skipped.

slides.title: The Infinite Loop ♾️
slides.content: You must be very careful with `while` loops! If the condition you provide *never* becomes false during the execution of the loop, the loop will run forever, crashing the browser or NodeJS server.

slides.title: Danger Zone
slides.content: If you forget to increment or change a variable, disaster strikes.
